$comment: D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL (TLS C1 x86_64 + C2 arm64 + C3 pe64 + C4 macho): single-thread thread_local witness — .tdata template copy visible before any write (g==7), mutation sticks, static thread_local (.tbss) accumulates from 0, thread_local const rides the same per-thread access. The release arm runs the full optimizer over the access sequence (the M-5 riprel-fold exclusions must hold under CSE/mem2reg). The arm64-ELF arm (TLS C2) runs the SAME source through the MRS TPIDR_EL0 + ADD/ADD hi12/lo12 Variant-I sequence under qemu-aarch64. The pe64 arm (TLS C3, runOn windows — LOCAL Windows ctest, no emulator) runs the SAME source through the gs:[0x58] TEB TLS-array + _tls_index + secrel sequence; pe64 embeds the whole per-thread block (incl. the .tbss zero region) in the .tls template because the Windows loader does not reliably zero SizeOfZeroFill for the main thread's early static TLS. The arm64-macho arm (TLS C4, runOn darwin) runs the SAME source through the __thread_vars descriptor + __tlv_bootstrap call sequence (adrp/add the descriptor -> ldr its thunk -> blr -> x0 = &var); the host CANNOT execute Mach-O, so this arm runs ONLY on the macos-latest CI leg / the user's Apple-Silicon Mac (structural pins in tests/link/test_macho_writer.cpp are the local guard). The per-thread discriminator lives in thread_local_pthread; this example's job is the loader-copies-template + mutation semantics. Exit 42.
